2025 TITAN Property Awards
WINNERS
JUDGES SPOTLIGHT
WORLDWIDE EXPERTS FROM DIVERSE PROFESSIONAL INDUSTRIES

Our esteemed jury panel consists of leading property industry professionals, whose contributions and innovations to the advancement of societal infrastructure are genuinely inspirational.

Showcase your innovative excellence and capture the attention of our esteemed panel of judges today!

United States

ARTEM KROPOVINSKY

Founder and Principal Designer, Arsight

 

Artem Kropovinsky, Founder of the New York–based design studio Arsight, is an accomplished interior designer with over a decade of experience leading transformative residential and commercial projects.

Recognition of Artem's prowess was unmistakably evident when he emerged as a finalist in a renowned international program. This acknowledgment underlines both his innovative spirit and his profound respect for cultural heritage in design. Always on the cutting edge, Artem champions the integration of the Metaverse, Virtual Reality (VR), and Augmented Reality (AR) in his projects. Such pioneering efforts have redefined client experiences, particularly for partners like Societhy.

Artem’s collaborations span from furniture makers like Design Galleria Kitchen and Bath Studio to tech innovators such as Virtual Staging Solutions, reflecting his holistic design approach. Known for blending history with modern aesthetics, his work on projects like the Weyanoke Hotel and Catbird Restaurants showcases his ability to create timeless spaces.

Netherlands

MISAK TERZIBASIYAN

Founder and Principal Architect, UArchitects

    

Misak Terzibasiyan is the founder and principal architect of UArchitects (Urban Design and Architects).

UArchitects is a global firm that embraces a diverse team of various nationalities. The founder, having studied at the Eindhoven University of Technology in The Netherlands, gained experience with several international architectural firms prior to establishing the company in the culturally and architecturally renowned city of Eindhoven.

Terzibasiyan's professional and personal pursuits are deeply rooted in a global perspective and the exploration of cultural motifs, a focus influenced by his own ethnic heritage, having been born in Helsinki. He has established himself as an esteemed individual in his domain, with a career marked by an international outlook and a dedication to incorporating cultural diversity into his work. His recognition in the field is a testament to his commitment to embracing and understanding the interplay of different cultures in his professional endeavors.

Portugal

TIAGO DO VALE

Senior Architect, Tiago do Vale Arquitectos

    

Tiago do Vale has contributed his remarkable skills as the senior architect at Tiago do Vale Arquitectos for a span of 15 years.

His extensive experience and creative vision contribute to the development of innovative design solutions that address clients' unique needs and aspirations. He plays a pivotal role in mentoring and guiding junior architects, fostering their professional growth and ensuring the successful execution of projects.

He was a Senator and Member of the University of Coimbra's Assembly and served as the contributing editor for the book "Urban Complex" published by Design Media Publishing, and its Chinese counterpart, "城市综合体," by Liaoning Science and Technology Publishing House. Do Vale’s published works have gained international recognition, and he has delivered lectures and authored articles on topics spanning architecture, urban planning, and rehabilitation in various locations.

View TITAN Property Jury Panel

If you’d like to be a part of our distinguished jury members, or be considered for future inclusions in the TITAN Awards program, do consider nominating yourself, and we’ll get back to you soon.

LIT ENTERTAINMENT AWARDS
Evolving with the Times – From Commercial to Entertainment.

LIT Entertainment Awards recognizes creative and advertising excellence in the global entertainment industry - from the silver screen to interactive gaming, from television content to wide-encompassing digital entertainments. Previously LIT Commercial Awards, LIT now transitions from its previous roots, expanding its reach to celebrate all facets of entertainment advertising and marketing, eagerly embracing this evolution to accommodate a broader spectrum of entertainment.

The LIT Entertainment Awards is open to all creative and marketing professionals, designers, agencies, and every creative in between. Whether you're reaching out to a local audience or making waves internationally, LIT is committed to spotlight advertising brilliance of entertainment on a grander stage than ever before!

ENTER LIT ENTERTAINMENT AWARDS

CATEGORIES
Celebrating the Spectrum of Creative Brilliance in Entertainment Across All Platforms Globally.

LIT Entertainment Awards salutes innovation, storytelling, and design, honoring an array of advertising and creative ideas that captivate audiences worldwide. From film, television, games, home, live or digital entertainment, music, and marketing, to the masterminds crafting these memorable experiences behind the scenes — LIT recognizes it all.

VIEW MORE LIT WINNERS
LITO STATUETTE
Icon of Entertainment Excellence

The LITO statuette stands as the emblematic heart of the LIT Entertainment Awards, radiating an enigmatic energy that signifies the boundless realm of entertainment advertising and marketing.

Dressed in its distinctive polka dot garb, LITO embodies the endless realms of imagination, encouraging participants across films, television, games, and all entertainment mediums to venture beyond the ordinary. It's a reminder to never lose sight of one's original goals, even as they craft fresh narratives and pioneering experiences.

LITO's base implements a refined and elegant finish, creating a stark contrast against its gleaming black and pristine white visage. It serves as a beacon, urging entertainment visionaries globally to shatter their own boundaries, aspiring not just to participate in the industry but to redefine it.

VIEW LITO STATUETTE

FEATURED JURORS

At the LIT Entertainment Awards, each and every submission is evaluated only by the best in the industry, comprising a collective of industry trailblazers and visionaries in the Grand Jury Panel. By presenting your masterpieces here, you're not just competing; you're sharing your ideas with some of the most esteemed minds in entertainment, impressing, inspiring, and resonating with an elite panel of individuals.

United States

Vasily Petrenko

CEO & Co-Founder, Another World VR Corporation

Vasily Petrenko is the CEO and co-founder of Another World VR Corp, a global leader in free-roam virtual reality experiences for location-based entertainment centers worldwide.

With a background in Cytology and Genetics from Novosibirsk State University, Vasily transitioned early into tech and entertainment, co-founding ventures that combine science, innovation, and storytelling. His journey began in 2014 with Time Quest Lab, supporting escape rooms, theme parks, and interactive exhibitions. As CFO, he managed forecasting, marketing, and growth, and at Another World, he has led expansion, rebranding, and capital raising while redefining how users engage with technology in physical spaces.

His expertise in strategy, investment, sales, and product–market fit, particularly within emerging tech sectors, gives him a sharp perspective for evaluating startups shaping the future.

United States

Jenn Szekely

Managing Partner, USA, Coley Porter Bell

Jenn leads Coley Porter Bell’s US business, based in New York and is a member of the Coley Porter Bell Board. She brings over 20 years of B2C and B2B branding and marketing experience across a variety of industries, including retail, technology, hospitality, FMCG, nonprofit, manufacturing and healthcare.

Prior to joining Coley Porter Bell, Jenn was Executive Director, Marketing & New Business for FutureBrand where she led the marketing and new business team in North America. As part of her role, she helped expand the business to the West Coast and worked with clients such as Cisco, Peloton, Honeywell, Tupperware, Hard Rock International, Microsoft and Forever 21.

Previously, Jenn was Senior Vice President at Siegel+Gale, working in both New York and London with clients such as PepsiCo, Target, Carter’s, J.Crew, Choice Hotels, Procter & Gamble, Sony, and Seamless GrubHub. A highlight of her time at Siegel+Gale was working on the branding of Jeff Bezos’ aerospace company, Blue Origin, where she had the opportunity to work with the Bezos family and learn about the amazing world of space travel.

Jenn’s experience spans a variety of strategic and creative initiatives, from brand strategy, brand architecture and naming to creative executions including, visual identity, packaging, digital and trade show experience to hotel and retail interior design.

Jenn has been a judge at the North American Transform Awards and before her career in branding, she ran her own company, which included two retail stores in Boston, Massachusetts, specializing in fashion, art, antiques and design that won numerous awards and received critical acclaim in national newspapers, magazines, books and television.

United Kingdom

Michael Boszko

Creative Director, Havas Play London

Michael Boszko is a Creative Director at Iris, creating work for leading cultural, sports, and entertainment brands.

Michael began his career in Dubai, writing for Nike, and has since garnered recognition at all major global festivals through his work with leading network agencies. Today, he leads Iris London’s Creative Council and also serves as a member of the agency’s Global Council, helping to shape creative vision and drive innovation across the network. His clients have included global names such as Samsung, Nike, HSBC, Pizza Hut, and Starbucks.

His expertise in cultural, sports, and entertainment branding makes him a discerning and insightful judge, well-positioned to evaluate work with both professional rigor and an appreciation for impactful storytelling.

Brazil

Nayla Alana

Associate Creative Director, LePub

Nayla Alana is an Associate Creative Director at LePub Brazil.
As an Associate Creative Director at LePub Brazil, Nayla crafts bold, insightful, and human-centred ideas that connect brands with audiences in meaningful ways. With over 14 years of experience, she brings a rich creative background shaped by her time at leading agencies such as Zulu Alpha Kilo Toronto, Galeria, Ogilvy Brazil, J. Walter Thompson, Publicis, and DDB Spain.

She is highly knowledgeable in brand communication with expertise spanning concept development, storytelling, and integrated campaign execution. She has crafted impactful campaigns for leading global brands, including Heineken, Jeep, TikTok, Adidas, Kotex, Ford, Nestlé, Netflix, Bradesco, Dove, and others.

If you’d like to consider playing a role in judging at the awards, or be considered for future inclusions in the LIT Entertainment Awards,
do submit a nomination and an award representative will reach out to you soon.

TITAN PROPERTY AWARDS

Calling all international property developers, interior designers, architects, landscape designers, lighting designers, and other property industry professionals.

Home is the place we stay and work at. No matter where we are, we are tied to our surrounding environments and its beautiful splendor, where life meets ingeniously built buildings and designs.

In TITAN Property Awards, we want to give each professional from the real estate industry a stage that continues to shine a spotlight onto their work for both country and environment. Your work highlights the best of both worlds: the ingenuity of the designers, and the efforts of every dedicated worker in this modernized industry. Celebrate your accolades with us. Build your splendor with a TITAN Property Awards win, today.

ENTRY INFORMATION

The TITAN Property Awards is designed for all professionals to enter online and upload their projects.

ELIGIBILITY
AWARDS PROCESS
REQUIREMENTS
WINNING LEVELS
JUDGING CRITERIA
WHY TITAN AWARDS

The awards program is open to all property developers, interior designers, architects, landscape designers and other property industry professionals. Any person, group, or organization may turn in their submissions online. To be eligible, an entry must have been produced after January 1, 2022. Entries may originate from any country.

ENTER TITAN PROPERTY AWARDS

DEADLINES & FEES

TITAN Property Awards makes every effort to keep entry costs accessible. Thus, our entry fees are $280 for all property development categories, $219 for all design / real estate categories, and $140 for all property marketing categories.

Rather than increasing the fee for each entry, fees are only charged in order to cover administrative costs like printing, mailing and data organization. For this reason, each participant is charged a flat $30 annual administrative fee. When all participants pay the same low fee, those submitting more than one entry will actually pay less than if fees were on a sliding scale.

TITAN PROPERTY AWARDS CATEGORIES

The TITAN Property Awards introduces categories dedicated to celebrating excellence in real estate, architecture, interior design, landscape, and property development. Created for designers and developers, each category recognizes outstanding achievement in design, functionality, and execution across residential, commercial, and mixed-use projects.

Explore the categories below and submit your most exceptional property design work today.

FAQ.text:
IAA Partners :
MUSE Awards 
Vega Awards
NYX Awards 
TITAN Awards 
NY Awards 
LIT Awards 
Noble Awards 
Arte Collection 
Tokyo Design Awards
FAQ
ENTRY SUBMISSION, ELIGIBILITIES AND CATEGORIES

How to submit entries?

Entry is simple. Submission is online and is completed in 5 simple steps:

Register - or login if you are already a registrant.
Fill in the simple online submission form - remember you can select multiple categories for each entry.
Upload supporting materials such as Image, Website links, PDF or Video links if needed.
Preliminary Checking Service (Property Development Category)
Add to Cart. Then, add more categories or checkout and pay.

Submissions

Established entries must be produced within the last FIVE (5) calendar years up through the final deadline.
All entries must be submitted for judging exactly as published or implemented and may not be modified for the competition. The TITAN Property Awards may request proof of initial production and / or publication date.
A written translation or adaptation must accompany non-English entries.
The award reserves the right to re-categorize incorrectly classified entries.
Judges reserve the right to withhold an award in a category if submissions do not meet their expectations.

What information do I need to provide to submit my project?

For submissions, you need to provide the following information:
Lead Interior Designer, Architect or Property Developer (required)
List of Team Members (optional)
Project Name (required)
Client (optional)
Category(s) (required)
Brief description of the project (required)
Upload images and/or PDF descriptions of the project
Add links to project (videos, online materials, website, etc) (optional)

Do I have to be based in the United States to participate?

No, The Property Awards is open to projects from all over the world.




Does my project have to be designed or developed in the US?

No, projects don’t have to be designed or developed within the US to participate in the competition. TITAN Property Awards is open to projects located anywhere in the world.




Who is eligible?

The TITAN Property Awards is open to: International builders, Interior Designers, Designers, Marketers, Agencies, Business Owner, Developers, Trade and Professional Associations, Public and Private Undertakings, Government and Semi-Government Departments, and even organizations and individual service providers and professionals who have made it big in the real estate sector can apply for this award. Even start ups and newcomers in the industry can participate by nominating in the "emerging" category. Sub-units of organizations are also eligible to apply. All applicants' information & details will be kept strictly confidential as per company regulations.




Do I have to be an Interior Designer, Architect or Property Developer to participate?

TITAN Property Awards welcomes creative thinkers from around the world to participate. You don’t have to be in one of the above categories to submit your project. You can also submit on behalf of your organization or individual.




How long should my entry brief be?

We recommend each synopsis to be kept at approximately 350 words. Do utilize this section to elaborate on the piece as well as providing the jury with a better understanding of your subject. A written translation or adaptation must accompany non-English entries.

Is there a limit to the number of entries I can submit?

There are no limits on the amount of entries you can submit.

How many images can I enter into the same category?

Up to 10 supporting images can be submitted with each project.

Can several projects be submitted together, or should I submit each project separately?

The jury will select the winners in each category based on individual projects, ie. not as an entire portfolio of projects, so each project should be submitted as a separate entry.

Does my project need to be completed to be submitted?

We accept completed, in progress and conceptual projects. Finished projects must be completed within the past five years.

Can I enter my work into multiple categories?

Yes, you can enter the same work into as many categories as you see fit. There will be an additional fee for each category.

Can I submit entries that have won an award, had been entered in a previous Property Awards competition, or in another competition?

Yes, submissions that have previously been submitted or have won an award are eligible to apply again this year. Most companies do participate again each year.

Can I send physical support materials for the competition? If so, will it be returned to me and do I need to send a return-handling fee?

You are not required to submit your drawings, floor plans or project plans. However, we encourage participants to submit as much information as possible for a better representation of your project. We understand that every project is unique, therefore, you have the freedom to submit any information that you think is suitable. We encourage you to provide a plan of at least one part of the project. This is to ensure our jury will have enough information for project evaluation.

How will I know if my submission is complete, and what do I do if it’s not?

If a submission is incomplete at the closing of the competition (e.g. the submission has not been paid, incorrectly uploaded files or insufficient descriptions), it will not be eligible.

Although we make every effort to ensure the completion of submissions, we are not responsible for the incompletion of entries. Thus, we are unable to offer a refund should an entry be incomplete at the time of the closing of the competition.

Can I make changes to my entry after I have paid?

Yes, you may edit your entry until the start of the judging period.

Submission Format

Image File

The submissions should be saved in a flattened JPEG or JPG format. (keep them under 4MB, 1,200px wide is preferable, 300dpi, RGB).




Audio/Video File

Upload video/audio to Vimeo/YouTube/Youku.
Paste the URL link into the URL field.

Entries with audio/video must be submitted with a direct URL that will take judges directly to the work. If it is password-protected, you must provide a username and password for judging purposes. Web Videos entered are judged solely on the content provided. Upload a PDF brief if explanation is required before evaluation of the work.

Mobile Sites/Apps

Mobile entries must be submitted with a direct URL and accessible by a mobile phone, PDA or other mobile devices as they will be evaluated on such devices. If password protected, you must provide a username and password for judging purposes. Mobile Websites are judged solely on the content provided. Additional supporting documents are not permitted. For Mobile Apps, submit a URL that leads to a demonstration of the application features and components, or a direct link for downloading.

What if I have trouble uploading my images

Firstly, check if the image does not exceed the maximum size (4MB) or width (1,200 px), and the file name only contains letters or numbers. Once checked, try to upload your images again. If you’re still having trouble after this, contact our team at info@thepropertyawards.com .

ENTRY FEE AND PAYMENT

Entry Fees

Regular Entry begins from $280 for all property development categories, $140 for all property marketing categories, and $219 for all design categories. If a project is submitted in more than one category, an additional submission fee for each category is required. Each new entrant is charged a $30 annual administrative fee.

Submissions fees will not be refundable once submitted. Withdrawn projects (whether withdrawn by entrant or by the Awards for the entrant’s failure to comply with entry rules) will NOT be refunded as well. Please click here to see entry fees.

Do I need to pay the submission fee before submitting the project?

No, submission fees are the last step in processing your payment.

How can I pay for the entry that I submit?

Online. You may pay for your entry after the submission of your works through Credit Card (VISA / MasterCard) or PayPal (AMEX / PayPal).

Are all the fees on your website in USD?

Yes, we only accept USD at this moment.

JUDGING

The juries

Our list of jurors consist of a wide variety of esteemed industry professionals, educators, influencers and editors who are devoted to creative professionals, marketing communications, interior design, architecture, and property development.

Judging criteria

Judging is blind. All entrant information is removed from entries to prevent bias/conflict of interest. Judges score on a scale of 1-100, based on the below criteria.

Each entry is evaluated on its own merit. It is not competing against other entries. The jury panel evaluates entries based on its creativity, form and function. The project needs to be clear on its relationship to its environment, its users, and its purpose. Creativity is not enough to be convincing; it needs to show useful function and provide a better living experience for its users, meeting the clients’ expectation. Projects that demonstrate innovation and embracing new technologies, setting new trends, respecting the environment, and pushing boundaries of human ingenuity. Projects with high regard to sustainable building practices and the protection and preservation of the environment will also earn special attention from the jury.

Entries receiving an average score under 50 do not receive an award.

The TITAN Property Awards and International Awards Associate Inc’s decision are final in its sole discretion and binding in relation to all matters relating to Competitions and may not be subject to any discussions or correspondence.

WINNERS

How do we choose TITAN Property Awards winners?

Submissions are judged at random without comparison to others in a category. Each project is evaluated on criteria determined by the requirements (see Judging Criteria). Individual are not compared to group in the same category.

To ensure high standards of high quality, a category may have multiple winners or no winners. Work is critiqued rated by consensus, with the panel of judges determining a rating on a scale of 100 points. Winners with a score between 85 and 100 points are Platinum Winners. Projects with a score between 70 and 84 points are Gold Winners, while projects with a score between 50 - 69 are Silver Winners.

One of the Highest score Platinum Winners for each main category will be selected as the Category Winner of the Year.

Projects receiving an average score under 50 do not receive an award.

Levels of winning and number of winning entries

There are FOUR (4) levels of achievement in the competition:

Category Winners of the Year (Only ONE highest score from each category will be recognized.)
Platinum - Average scores between 85 - 100
Gold - Average scores between 70 - 84
Silver - Average scores between 50 - 69

The number of awards presented by TITAN Property Awards is determined by the number of submissions in a given category, the scoring range, and the number of tie scores.

Note: It is not necessary for each category to have a winning title for the Category Winners of the Year. The judges decision is final, and the selections are based entirely on the overall quality of submissions in the given category.

How do I know if I’ve won an award?

All 2026 winners will be announced on May 15, 2026 via TITAN Property Awards official page. All registered participants can then log in to see the winning works.

We will duly notify all award winners by email. Do ensure that your email address is correct, as our notifications may get caught in spam filters or receive a non-deliverable return. You may change your email address by logging into your account and updating your information.

What do the winners receive?

Each year, the TITAN Property Awards honors the best in property from around the globe, while you give us a glimpse into the future of the profession

One complimentary TITAN statuette will be given to all Category Winners of the Year.
One professionally designed certificate commemorating the winning entry, which includes entry title, entering company/winners' name and category. Decal will be affixed indicating the level of win.
All winners will be featured on TITAN Property Awards site's Gallery, where professionals and public can view your work.
A press release sent to major industry outlets, celebrating your win.
Professionally designed logos for use on websites, in email signatures, etc.
In addition to the complimentary certificates, Platinum and Gold Winners may purchase beautiful TITAN Property Awards statuettes, and/or additional customized certificates.
Category Winners of the Year and Platinum Winners will get featured by TITAN Property Awards on social media or our media partner on Muse.World’s website.

Why must winner’s trophies be purchased?

The TITAN Property Awards’ mission is to recognize outstanding achievement in firms that otherwise are overshadowed by large firms' budgets, which dominate other property awards programs.

The affordable submission fees allow start-ups, one-person firms and young individuals the opportunity to enter, as well as all entrepreneurs from countries where the exchange rate or the economic conditions would otherwise make entering impossible.

If an entrant is recognized as a winner, there is no additional fee. Many other awards programs charge an acceptance fee. With TITAN Property Awards, once you've won, you've won; there is no additional fee.

If a winner decides to purchase a statuette, the TITAN Property Awards makes them available by ordering them from our manufacturer. This offering process allows the Awards to maintain low entry fees and prevent non-winner’s subsidy of winners.

Some winners choose not to receive a physical memento. Their priority is the prestige of recognition and the exposure the comes with it. Others choose to order statuettes for individuals on their team as well as their client. It's all up to the winners!

Statuette shipping time

Winner awards are custom-made. Statuettes will ship directly from the manufacturer. Please allow 6 - 12 weeks for the statuettes. Shipping time may be longer during off-season. Statuettes may be ordered from our online store once winners are announced.

Note: Certain countries might impose customs / duty fees and taxes for imported items. We have no control over these charges, and we suggest to consult your country's customs department.

We strongly recommend recording a video while opening your package, including any enclosed certificate documents. This video is essential in the event you need to file a replacement or damage claim. Without this, we may not be able to proceed with your request.

GENERAL

The TITAN Property Awards sponsors?

The TITAN Property Awards is conducted by International Awards Associate Inc (IAA). IAA is not tied to any advertiser, magazine, trade association, a

TAKE YOUR EXCLUSIVE SEAT ON THE
TITAN PROPERTY AWARDS PANEL

Our jury panel consists of leading real estate professionals, whose talents and contributions to both nation and environment are instrumental to building a well-constructed community. These individuals pave the way towards modern constructions and architectures, being an integral part of a new modernized society.

We would like to thank our highly esteemed jurors for their dedication, passion, and hard work in adjudicating these wonderful projects. This awards program would not be possible without the help of these enthusiastic professionals.

Filter by year
2026
2025
2024
2023
2022
2021

United States

ARTEM KROPOVINSKY

Founder and Principal Designer, Arsight

 

Artem Kropovinsky, Founder of the New York–based design studio Arsight, is an accomplished interior designer with over a decade of experience leading transformative residential and commercial projects.

Recognition of Artem's prowess was unmistakably evident when he emerged as a finalist in a renowned international program. This acknowledgment underlines both his innovative spirit and his profound respect for cultural heritage in design. Always on the cutting edge, Artem champions the integration of the Metaverse, Virtual Reality (VR), and Augmented Reality (AR) in his projects. Such pioneering efforts have redefined client experiences, particularly for partners like Societhy.

Artem’s collaborations span from furniture makers like Design Galleria Kitchen and Bath Studio to tech innovators such as Virtual Staging Solutions, reflecting his holistic design approach. Known for blending history with modern aesthetics, his work on projects like the Weyanoke Hotel and Catbird Restaurants showcases his ability to create timeless spaces.

Netherlands

MISAK TERZIBASIYAN

Founder and Principal Architect, UArchitects

    

Misak Terzibasiyan is the founder and principal architect of UArchitects (Urban Design and Architects).

UArchitects is a global firm that embraces a diverse team of various nationalities. The founder, having studied at the Eindhoven University of Technology in The Netherlands, gained experience with several international architectural firms prior to establishing the company in the culturally and architecturally renowned city of Eindhoven.

Terzibasiyan's professional and personal pursuits are deeply rooted in a global perspective and the exploration of cultural motifs, a focus influenced by his own ethnic heritage, having been born in Helsinki. He has established himself as an esteemed individual in his domain, with a career marked by an international outlook and a dedication to incorporating cultural diversity into his work. His recognition in the field is a testament to his commitment to embracing and understanding the interplay of different cultures in his professional endeavors.

Portugal

TIAGO DO VALE

Senior Architect, Tiago do Vale Arquitectos

    

Tiago do Vale has contributed his remarkable skills as the senior architect at Tiago do Vale Arquitectos for a span of 15 years.

His extensive experience and creative vision contribute to the development of innovative design solutions that address clients' unique needs and aspirations. He plays a pivotal role in mentoring and guiding junior architects, fostering their professional growth and ensuring the successful execution of projects.

He was a Senator and Member of the University of Coimbra's Assembly and served as the contributing editor for the book "Urban Complex" published by Design Media Publishing, and its Chinese counterpart, "城市综合体," by Liaoning Science and Technology Publishing House. Do Vale’s published works have gained international recognition, and he has delivered lectures and authored articles on topics spanning architecture, urban planning, and rehabilitation in various locations.

United States

ALICE LI

Director, Cultural Strategy and Innovation, sparks and honey (Omnicom Group)

 

Alice Li is the Director of Cultural Strategy and Innovation at sparks & honey (Omnicom Group), a consultancy that uses tech-driven cultural intelligence to help organizations understand and stay ahead of global shifts.

Li is the trusted authority on future frameworks and spearheads the majority of future projects at sparks & honey. As a quantitative futurist with a proven track record in foresight and innovation strategy, Li has advised numerous Fortune 100 C-suite executives, aiding in their decision-making endeavors.

Beyond her role in management consulting, she holds a distinguished position as a judge for international design awards. Her goal is to provide the design community with a rich array of cultural perspectives, empowering the next generation to make informed, forward-thinking design decisions. This, in turn, prepares them to be responsible and thoughtful contributors to society, making impactful contributions to the world.

Netherlands

ASBJOERK STANLY MOGENSEN (BRIAN HOEHL)

Chief Executive Officer and Design Director, Bikedezign

Brian Hoehl holds the positions of Chief Executive Officer and Design Director at BikedeZign. Additionally, he has played roles as an investor, product manager, market intelligence director, brand manager, HDC design director, prototype specialist, director of innovation, micro-mobility expert, and licensed brand specialist.

With a decade of experience in global product development and design, Hoehl has garnered more than 200 International Design Awards. He's credited as the innovator behind the original downtube battery design, now recognized as Bosch power tubes. For the past 18 years, he's partnered with various automotive companies to develop e-mobility vehicles and pioneer advancements in micromobility.

He's a pioneering figure in the bicycle industry, celebrated for his award-winning work. He acts as the link that binds the brand identity of top bike brands to the industrial design that maintains product line consistency. His primary mission revolves around brand-building through design thinking processes.

United Kingdom

BEN DICKINSON

Broker and Advisor, ME Developments

Ben Dickinson boasts a remarkable career as a broker and advisor for luxury real estate agencies, complemented by an impressive sales track record. He is a notable figure in the United Kingdom's property market.

Dickinson possesses extensive expertise in the home counties and London real estate markets. As the Group Sales Director for ME Developments and an Advisory Director for Prime Partnership, Estate Agents, he has a deep grasp of the property sector. Known for his dedication, he consistently aims for favorable outcomes in all scenarios. He is a professionally reliable enabling his clients to fulfill their objectives and requirements.

He is well-recognized for his dedication and responsible approach to handling clients' valuable assets. He focuses on innovative solutions and breaking records to achieve results and his numerous awards underscore his professional achievements. He is also notable for his charitable contributions, having raised over £370,000 for Cancer Research.

Spain

BENJAMIN CALLEJA

CEO and Founder, Livit Design and Fast Fine Restaurant Group

 

Benjamin Calleja serves as the CEO of Livit Design, the founder of Fast Fine Restaurant Group and is also a Partner within the Passionality Group.

As the CEO of the world's largest restaurant design company, his primary focus revolves around enhancing brand performance through the design of guest experiences. He has a global presence, actively working in 40 countries across five continents, where a new restaurant is created approximately every 8 hours.

He is a recognized global authority in the field of hospitality design and technology, often sought after as a speaker at conferences, events, and masterclasses. Additionally, he holds prestigious memberships in organizations such as The American Institute of Architects and is a registered architect in multiple European countries including Germany, France, Italy, the United Kingdom, Spain, Luxembourg, and Sweden.

United States

BO ZHANG

Founder, Desz Office

 

Bo Zhang , Artist, Designer and Curator . founded Desz Office, a creative studio that mixes art, design, material and communications to balance art and design, expresses the relationship between people and space, stimulates people's imagination of contemporary art and design. Bo also is initiator of the RE-CRAFT project. The goal of RE-CRAFT is to integrate art, design, craftsmanship and culture to give culture and craftsmanship new vitality.

"Make creations that have never been seen before" is Desz's unchanging philosophy, which reflects his commitment to pushing the boundaries of creativity. He believes that creativity and originality are the bedrock of his work, allowing it to be loved by those who experience it. Desz holds that a true masterpiece should evoke sentiment, possessing a soul that fosters a meaningful connection with people. This connection goes beyond the visual, reaching into a deeper, spiritual realm that inspires and stirs a sense of yearning in the viewer.

China

CATHY CHOU

Founder, Vaster Creative Cultural Consulting Co.,Ltd

Cathy Chou, boasting a remarkable 13 years of expertise in design, brand strategy, and marketing, is the visionary founder behind Vaster Creative Cultural Consulting Co., Ltd.

Over the years as an employee in renowned firms, she has provided top-notch creative interior design planning and expert advice to various clients, including luxury resort hotels, renowned real estate companies, tourist destinations, government bodies, and elite businesses. Her clients span diverse sectors, including five-star hotels, upscale office complexes, exclusive clubs, innovative offices, retail brand showrooms, customized support clubs, and attractive sales centers.

In addition, she specializes in overseeing the branding and management of design firms, along with offering marketing and strategic planning services to Fortune 500 clients. Chou's multifaceted background encompasses brand strategy, brand management, marketing, and planning where she has gained extensive expertise in marketing and brand management within prominent real estate marketing, planning, and design firms.

Taiwan

CHING WEN HUANG

Design Director, D.H.I.A International Design CO.,Ltd

Ching Wen Huang serves as the very capable Director at D.H.I.A International Design, leveraging her professionalism and extensive experience to breathe vitality into living environments.

Huang's multifaceted role as a creative design director encompasses not only the cultivation of creative excellence within her teams but also the facilitation of seamless interdisciplinary collaboration, prudent financial stewardship, and the preservation of design congruity. These combined magnificent qualities underscore Huang's indispensable role as a dynamic and charismatic leader, skillfully uniting creative brilliance with strategic acumen, making a significant impact in the realm of design which brilliantly depicts her commitment to the industry.

Huang's commitment to prioritizing the quality of life and value in her design philosophy not only reflects her passion for meaningful and impactful creations but has also earned her a well-deserved reputation as a distinguished design director, celebrated and awarded for her outstanding contributions to the industry.

Canada

CHRISTINA KUO

Creative Director, KUUO LIVING

 

Christina Kuo, currently serving as the creative director at Kuuo Living, brings several years of valuable experience from her work in the hospitality and residential design sectors.

Kuo possesses a versatile understanding of various design styles, enabling her to tailor her approach to suit the specific needs of each project. Her creative acumen has allowed her to skillfully engage in a diverse range of exciting ventures. She is wondrously adept at crafting bespoke design strategies that align precisely with the distinctive demands and complexities of each project, all while meeting the preferences of her clients flawlessly.

Her diverse international experience offers a unique perspective to her work, encompassing all facets of hospitality projects from concept to implementation. Her multilingual skills and extensive design background make her a versatile and seasoned talent, with expertise spanning luxury hotels, resorts, spas, retail, restaurants, and high-end residential spaces.


United States

CHUN LIU

Senior Associate, CO Architects

 

Chun Liu is a Senior Associate and Project Architect at CO Architects in Los Angeles where she leads the project team on a variety of projects ranging from healthcare, k-12, and higher education.

Over the past 15 years, she has contributed to various award-winning projects. Her journey in pursuit of enhancing human well-being through design and research took root in 2017 when she worked at Perkins & Will, Boston, where she embarked on the planning and design of healthcare projects. Understanding the unique nature of healthcare design and the impact of the built environment on our lives, she is dedicated to creating distinctive solutions that incorporate principles of humanity and sustainability into every facet of design.

Chun Liu earned her master's degree in architecture from Washington University in St. Louis with a Degree Project Award.

Japan

DAISUKE NAGATOMO

Founder and Associate Professor, MisoSoupDesign

  

Daisuke Nagatomo serves as the founder and director of MisoSoupDesign, and he is a Japanese designer and educator.

His educational background at Meiji University in Tokyo and subsequent architectural studies at Columbia University have equipped him with a strong foundation in design principles and a global perspective, allowing him to infuse innovative and culturally informed elements into the projects undertaken by MisoSoupDesign. He participated as one of the invited researchers in the FabLab House project at IaaC in Barcelona from 2009 to 2010, earning a research diploma during his tenure.

In his various teaching positions at these diverse institutions, Nagatomo has played a pivotal role in shaping the educational experiences of students. Beyond imparting valuable design and architectural knowledge, he actively fosters an environment for cross-cultural exchanges and the sharing of innovative ideas among students from different backgrounds and perspectives.

Russia

EKATERINA ELIZAROVA

Owner, Elizarova Design Studio

     

Ekaterina Elizarova is an international designer, winner of the world's leading awards, including Red Dot, A'Design, Outstanding Property Award London and many others.

Ekaterina Elizarova is the first Russian product designer in the Metaverse.

In 2008, Ekaterina founded her own studio, which develops unique author's coll

TITAN PROPERTY AWARDS

Calling all international property developers, interior designers, architects, landscape designers, lighting designers, and other property industry professionals.

Home is the place we stay and work at. No matter where we are, we are tied to our surrounding environments and its beautiful splendor, where life meets ingeniously built buildings and designs.

In TITAN Property Awards, we want to give each professional from the real estate industry a stage that continues to shine a spotlight onto their work for both country and environment. Your work highlights the best of both worlds: the ingenuity of the designers, and the efforts of every dedicated worker in this modernized industry. Celebrate your accolades with us. Build your splendor with a TITAN Property Awards win, today.

ELIGIBILITY
AWARDS PROCESS
REQUIREMENTS
WINNING LEVELS
JUDGING CRITERIA
WHY TITAN AWARDS

The awards program is open to all property developers, interior designers, architects, landscape designers and other property industry professionals. Any person, group, or organization may turn in their submissions online. To be eligible, an entry must have been produced after January 1, 2022. Entries may originate from any country.

ENTER TITAN PROPERTY AWARDS

DEADLINES & FEES

TITAN Property Awards makes every effort to keep entry costs accessible. Thus, our entry fees are $280 for all property development categories, $219 for all design / real estate categories, and $140 for all property marketing categories.

Rather than increasing the fee for each entry, fees are only charged in order to cover administrative costs like printing, mailing and data organization. For this reason, each participant is charged a flat $30 annual administrative fee. When all participants pay the same low fee, those submitting more than one entry will actually pay less than if fees were on a sliding scale.
